violations.text: The establishment does not employ a full-time certified food safety manager that is registered with the RIDOH Center for Food Protection. Application printed for PIC at the time of inspection. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 12/31/2024

The ice scoop is stored on an unclean surface (on the wire shelf) between uses. During pauses in food preparation, utensils must be stored on a clean surface or in a clean, protected location. Scoop was placed in plastic food container for storage at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 12/31/2024

The scoop used for sugar was observed to be stored on a shelf of the wire shelving unit next to the ice machine. Utensils used to dispense non-time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods such as sugar and flour must be stored in the food item with their handles above the top of the food within the container. Scoop was properly stored at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 12/31/2024

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety with the RIDOH Center for Food Protection at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. New Violation. Correct By: 12/12/2023

Egg bites were observed thawing under the oven on the speed racks. PIC moved food to thaw in refrigeration at the time of inspection. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods must be thawed in one of the following manners: under refrigeration (41 degrees Fahrenheit or less); completely submerged under running water; as part of the cooking process; or in the microwave if cooked immediately after thawing. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/28/2023
